segments.text: When Jacob found out grain was available in Egypt, he asked his sons,
Why do you keep on looking at each other to do something?
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: I've heard there's grain in Egypt. Go there and buy some for us so we can stay alive—if not, we're going to die!
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: So ten of Joseph's brothers went to Egypt to buy grain.
But Jacob did not send Joseph's brother Benjamin with his other brothers, for he said,
I'm afraid something bad might happen to him.
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: So Israel's sons went to buy grain along with everyone else, because there was famine in Canaan too.
Joseph was the governor of the country and he sold grain to all the people there. So Joseph's brothers went to him, and bowed low before him with their faces to the ground.
Joseph recognized them as soon as he saw them, but he acted like a stranger towards them and spoke to them in a severe way, saying,
Where are you from?
segments.speaker: Joseph

segments.text: From the country of Canaan,
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: they replied.
We've come to buy food.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Even though Joseph recognized his brothers, they didn't recognize him.
Joseph thought back to the dreams he'd had about them, and told them,
No! You're spies! You've come to discover our country's weaknesses!
segments.speaker: Joseph

segments.text: That's not true, my lord!
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: they responded.
We, your servants, have just come to buy food.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: We're all the sons of one man and we're honest. We're not spies!
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: No! You've come to find our country's weaknesses!
segments.speaker: Joseph

segments.text: he insisted.
Your servants are twelve brothers, the sons of one man living in the country of Canaan,
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: they explained.
The youngest is right now with our father, and one has passed away.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: As I said before, you're spies!
segments.speaker: Joseph

segments.text: Joseph declared.
This is how your story will be checked. I swear on Pharaoh's life that you'll never leave this country unless your younger brother comes here.
segments.speaker: Joseph

segments.text: One of you go back and bring your other brother here. The others of you will be kept here in prison until it's clear that you're telling the truth. If not, then I swear on Pharaoh's life it proves you're spies!
segments.speaker: Joseph

segments.text: So Joseph put all of them in prison for three days.
On the third day he told them,
Since I'm someone who respects God, do as I tell you and you'll live.
segments.speaker: Joseph

segments.text: If you're truly honest, choose one of your brothers to stay here in prison. The rest of you can go back home with grain for your hungry families.
segments.speaker: Joseph

segments.text: But you must bring your youngest brother here to me to prove what you're saying is true. If not, you will all die.
segments.speaker: Joseph

segments.text: They agreed to do this.
Clearly we're being punished for what we did to our brother,
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: they said to each other.
We watched him in agony pleading with us for mercy, but we refused to listen to him. That's why we're in all this trouble.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Reuben said to them,
Didn't I tell you, ‘Don't harm the boy!’ But you didn't listen to me. Now we're paying the price for what we did to him.
segments.speaker: Reuben

segments.text: They didn't realize that Joseph understood what they were saying because they were talking to him through an interpreter.
Joseph stepped away from them because he started crying. He came back when he was able to speak to them again. He chose Simeon and had him tied up as they watched.

Joseph gave the order to fill up their sacks with grain, and also to return the money they had paid by placing it in the sacks as well. He also ordered that they should be provided with food for their journey home. All this was done.
The brothers loaded the grain onto their donkeys and then set off.
On their way they stopped for the night, and one of them opened up his sack to give his donkey something to eat and saw his money there at the top of the sack.
He told his brothers,
My money's been returned to me. It's right here at the top of my sack!
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: They were horrified! Trembling with fear they asked each other,
What is this that God's done to us?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: When they arrived home in Canaan, they told their father Jacob everything that had happened.
The man who is the country's governor spoke to us in a severe way, and accused us of spying on the land,
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: they explained.
We told him, ‘We are honest men. We're not spies!
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: We are twelve brothers, the sons of one father. One has passed away and the youngest is right now with our father in the country of Canaan.’
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Then the man who is the country's governor said to us, ‘This is how I'll find out if you're telling the truth: you are to leave one of your brothers here with me while the rest take grain home for your hungry families.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Then bring your youngest brother to me. That way I'll know you're not spies but you're telling the truth. I'll release your brother to you, and you can stay in the country and trade.’
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: As they emptied their sacks, each one's money bag was there in his sack! When they and their father saw the money bags, they were horrified.
Jacob their father accused them,
You have taken Joseph from me—he's gone! Simeon is gone too! Now you want to take Benjamin away! I'm the one who's suffering from all of this!
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: You can kill my two sons if I don't bring him back to you,
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Reuben assured him.
Trust me with him, and I will bring him home to you myself.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: My son won't go there with you!
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: Jacob declared.
His brother is dead, and he's the only one I have left. If anything bad happens to him on the journey you're planning, you'll send this old man to his grave in grief.
segments.speaker: Jacob
